title: teucrium divaricatum subsp. divaricatum
intro: Teucrium divaricatum subsp. divaricatum, also known as the Woodland Germander, is a subspecies of plant in the mint family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Woodland Germander include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health concerns. Woodland Germander can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
